Summary: Eliminating strict Debt-to-Income (DTI) barriers and the heavy emotional anxiety of carrying double monthly mortgage payments simultaneously.
Purpose: Leverage a guaranteed backup agreement to completely remove your existing mortgage debt from your DTI calculation.
Client Fees & Costs: 1.00% of the guaranteed purchase price plus a flat $2,000 transaction setup fee. These costs are deferred and paid out of closing proceeds when the old home sells.
Benefit: 100% of open-market equity upside stays completely in your pocket.
Property Restrictions: Standard single-family detached homes, townhomes, and warranted condos. Excludes manufactured, modular, unique historical, or agricultural land.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (King, Snohomish, Pierce counties fully covered)
Rocket Operational Integration: Calque issues a legally binding Guaranteed Backup Agreement (GBA). Rocket Mortgage uses this contract to completely exclude the old home's mortgage debt from the client's Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio, allowing seamless qualification for the new purchase loan.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Register an agent profile on the Calque portal (calqueinc.com/agents). No extensive certification required, but you must introduce your client's Rocket Loan Officer to Calque's onboarding team to sync portals.
Agent Payout & Commissions: You retain 100% of full negotiated commissions on both sides (listing side of the old house and buying side of the new house). Calque charges zero referral fees to the agent or brokerage.
MLS Listing Window (Days): Up to 150 to 180 days to sell the old property vacant on the open market before the Calque backup offer is triggered.
Resale Profit Policy: Net Profit Return Guarantee: If Calque is forced to execute the backup offer and buy the home, any net profit from their subsequent resale is returned completely to your client.
Back-End Operational Flow: Calque operates behind the scenes using warehouse credit facilities to guarantee the backup purchase, completely absorbing the secondary title risk so outside lenders can qualify the buyer.

Summary: Turning a traditional financing offer into a non-contingent cash-equivalent offer to confidently win intense bidding wars in competitive markets.
Purpose: Provides a short-term cash bridge to buy your next home before selling your current one ensuring non-contingent buying power.
Client Fees & Costs: Variable short-term convenience and administrative fees depending on the selected bridge track (typically ranges between 1% and 2% of the transaction volume).
Benefit: Allows you to secure your next home safely without immediate home-sale contingencies while retaining your choice of long-term mortgage layouts.
Property Restrictions: Must meet traditional conforming loan property profiles within active NWMLS boundaries. Highly unique or severely distressed homes are excluded.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (Corporate headquarters in Seattle; deeply integrated)
Rocket Operational Integration: Flyhomes operates strictly as a wholesale provider. They fund the short-term cash bridge loan or purchase money bridge. Rocket Mortgage is brought in on day one to handle the long-term, permanent takeout mortgage.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Request agent credentials via the Flyhomes Agent Portal. You must participate in a brief platform walkthrough and link the client's external Rocket Loan Officer to the deal file before submitting the property for approval.
Agent Payout & Commissions: You retain your full client relationship. You collect 100% of your traditional listing and buying commissions. Flyhomes acts strictly behind the scenes as a wholesale transaction partner.
MLS Listing Window (Days): Typically 90 to 120 days to market the old home on the open MLS to pay off the wholesale bridge capital.
Resale Profit Policy: N/A. The client controls the open-market sale to clear the bridge; if the backup safety contract is triggered, Flyhomes retains subsequent holding profits.
Back-End Operational Flow: Flyhomes serves as a pure wholesale liquidity conduit, utilizing corporate capital pools to clear contingencies for external agents without creating consumer lending competition.

Summary: Navigating rigid corporate relocation deadlines or avoiding the stress of public open houses constant staging upkeep and buyer foot traffic.
Purpose: Provides an immediate guaranteed cash backstop offer on your current residence allowing you to bypass public open houses entirely.
Client Fees & Costs: A standard 5.00% service fee, plus variable deductions for corporate-assessed repairs following their institutional property inspection.
Benefit: Offers a guaranteed sale date with maximum scheduling flexibility eliminating market timing risks entirely.
Property Restrictions: Built after 1950, maximum of 1-2 acres, clear title, and valued under localized corporate price caps. No major foundation, fire, or structural defects.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (Major coverage across Greater Seattle Area/NWMLS)
Rocket Operational Integration: This is an outright cash sale of the departing home. The seller receives clean cash proceeds and walks away completely untethered, allowing them to use Rocket Mortgage for any future purchase without restrictions.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Create and verify an account on the 'Opendoor for Agents' portal. You must submit the property address, basic features, and photos to pull a preliminary corporate cash backstop offer before listing on the NWMLS.
Agent Payout & Commissions: Opendoor pays a 1% broker-to-broker referral fee directly to Redfin at closing. You can also charge your seller a standard representation or consulting fee via your Redfin listing agreement.
MLS Listing Window (Days): The corporate cash offer is valid for 14 to 30 days, giving you a tight window to test the open market before executing the backstop.
Resale Profit Policy: Opendoor retains 100% of all future resale profits or appreciation after taking ownership of the property.
Back-End Operational Flow: Uses algorithmic iBuying underwriting to instantly purchase properties into corporate inventory, managing institutional holding pipelines for rapid turnaround sales.

Summary: Securing an immediate guaranteed exit from a distressed inherited or high-maintenance property without performing cleaning or repairs.
Purpose: Programmatically routes your property details to an extensive network of pre-vetted private equity cash buyers and property flippers.
Client Fees & Costs: No direct program fee. The cost is reflected in the investor's cash offer which is typically discounted compared to full open-market retail value.
Benefit: Allows an immediate asset liquidation in as little as 10 days completely as-is skipping repairs staging and the public MLS.
Property Restrictions: Highly flexible. Accepts distressed homes, unbacked condos, hoarding situations, and unique structural properties rejected by standard iBuyers.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (Widespread investor network across WA state)
Rocket Operational Integration: The property is liquidated directly to a cash investor. The seller exits with pure cash proceeds, freeing them up to work immediately with Rocket Mortgage on their subsequent property purchase.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Sign up for a standard HomeLight Agent Account. Navigate to the Simple Sale dashboard within your portal to submit property details, disclosures, and interior conditions to initiate investor bidding.
Agent Payout & Commissions: Allows you to represent the seller traditionally. You retain the listing commission structured in your agreement, which is paid directly out of the institutional buyer's closing proceeds.
MLS Listing Window (Days): N/A. Avoids the MLS completely. Offers are generated within 48 hours, and closing can occur in as little as 10 days.
Resale Profit Policy: Institutional investors and flippers retain 100% of all future profits generated after renovating and reselling the property.
Back-End Operational Flow: Operates a programmatic routing matrix that instantly matches property parameters against localized criteria of thousands of private equity and institutional flippers.

Summary: Unlocking deep equity pools trapped in the current residence to secure a down payment for a new home before the old house is sold.
Purpose: Advances up to 90% of your current home's equity to use immediately as a down payment on your next purchase layout.
Client Fees & Costs: 2.40% of the old home's final sale price when utilizing an outside lender like Rocket Mortgage (penalized; drops to 1.70% if using HomeLight Home Loans).
Benefit: Enables strong non-contingent buying power with full upside protection—if the home resells for more you keep the remaining net profit.
Property Restrictions: Owner-occupied primary single-family residences or townhomes. Must meet strict equity threshold requirements following the HomeLight valuation.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (Fully functional in WA state)
Rocket Operational Integration: HomeLight advances up to 90% of old equity. Rocket Mortgage is permitted to write the new purchase loan, but HomeLight increases its convenience fee to penalize the client for using an outside lender.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Create a HomeLight agent profile. Submit a formal, exhaustive Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) and property condition report through the portal to establish the initial 'Equity Unlock' threshold.
Agent Payout & Commissions: You retain 100% of both your standard listing and buying commissions. HomeLight does not take a cut of your commission for the Buy Before You Sell program.
MLS Listing Window (Days): 90 to 120 days to market the old property vacant on the open market before HomeLight steps in to purchase it via the backup contract.
Resale Profit Policy: Upside Protection Clause: If HomeLight executes the backup purchase and later flips the property for a higher amount, they return all remaining net profit to your client.
Back-End Operational Flow: Utilizes dedicated bridge funding facilities to advance down payment liquid capital directly into the closing escrow of the buyer's new property purchase.

Summary: Rescuing a pending listing sale from total collapse when an active buyer's traditional financing falls through right before the closing date.
Purpose: Trio steps in as an institutional cash buyer to purchase the home converting the transaction into a long-term lease-option contract for the occupant.
Client Fees & Costs: Zero direct cost or discount fees to the seller. Trio buys the home at the agreed open-market price. The buyer/tenant absorbs all of Trio's program initiation and option fees.
Benefit: Sellers secure a clean cash exit at the full original contract price while buyers secure an alternative path to eventual homeownership.
Property Restrictions: Property must meet standard FHA-style safety, soundness, and habitability guidelines. Must be located within active Trio county limits in WA.
Seattle / WA Availability: Highly Active (Headquartered in Bellevue; premier market footprint)
Rocket Operational Integration: Trio steps in as an institutional cash buyer to purchase your listing when an interested buyer fails traditional financing. The seller gets a clean cash exit, and can use Rocket for their next purchase.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Complete the Trio Agent Training Webinar or sign up on their partner broker portal. You must understand their lease-to-own terms before presenting a Trio cash contract to a distressed or unqualified buyer looking at your listing.
Agent Payout & Commissions: You collect your full listing side commission as negotiated in your original listing agreement, paid out directly from Trio's corporate cash purchase funds at close of escrow.
MLS Listing Window (Days): Standard escrow timeline. Once Trio signs the cash purchase contract to rescue the deal, escrow typically closes within 21 to 30 days.
Resale Profit Policy: N/A. Trio purchases and holds the property long-term as an institutional landlord for the lease-to-own occupant.
Back-End Operational Flow: Functions as an institutional single-family housing fund, deploying corporate capital to buy retail listings and execute simultaneous long-term lease-option contracts.

Summary: Overcoming low buyer demand caused by high market interest rates by leveraging a highly attractive low-rate historic pandemic-era mortgage asset.
Purpose: Markets and processes the legal assumption of your low-rate primary FHA or VA mortgage asset to attract a wider pool of motivated buyers.
Client Fees & Costs: Zero cost to the seller. The home buyer pays a flat 1.00% transaction coordination fee directly to Roam at closing to manage the assumption process.
Benefit: Allows sellers to unlock a premium listing advantage by advertising an interest rate far below current open-market trends.
Property Restrictions: The underlying loan must be a government-backed FHA or VA mortgage containing a valid assumption clause. Property must be the buyer's primary residence.
Seattle / WA Availability: Active (Covering all eligible FHA/VA properties in WA)
Rocket Operational Integration: The buyer assumes the seller's low-rate primary FHA/VA loan. Rocket Mortgage cannot write the primary loan because it is being transferred, but Rocket can write the 'second mortgage' or equity gap loan to cover the buyer's down payment shortage.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: Register an account on Roam's agent portal. You must complete a brief training module regarding the legalities of FHA/VA loan assumptions and sign a partner agent framework agreement to receive operational support.
Agent Payout & Commissions: You retain your full traditional commission from the sales contract. Roam does not cut your commission; they charge their transaction coordination fee directly to the home buyer at closing.
MLS Listing Window (Days): Takes 45 to 90 days to process the assumption through the seller's current mortgage servicer on the open market.
Resale Profit Policy: N/A. This is a traditional retail transfer; your seller receives 100% of their equity at the initial closing table.
Back-End Operational Flow: Deploys specialized closing coordinators who bypass front-line customer service to deal directly with the specialized assumption departments of major mortgage servicers.

Summary: Maximizing the final open-market sales price of a property with deferred maintenance when the seller lacks out-of-pocket cash for upgrades.
Purpose: Floats up to 100% of cosmetic pre-sale home renovation costs with zero out-of-pocket interest or project management fees.
Client Fees & Costs: 0% interest and $0 project management fees. The client pays only the exact, raw contractor invoices for the renovations, with the entire balance deferred to closing.
Benefit: 100% of the valuation upside and premium market profit generated by the upgrades goes directly into your pocket at closing.
Property Restrictions: Must be an active, exclusive Redfin listing contract. Renovation budgets are capped based on a conservative estimation of the home's net equity position.
Seattle / WA Availability: Fully Active (Standard internal Redfin offering for WA listings)
Rocket Operational Integration: Financing is completely restricted to the sell-side listing process. It is entirely independent of the buy side and has zero overlap or restrictions on using Rocket Mortgage for a future purchase.
Mandatory Agent Onboarding Steps: No external onboarding required. Simply log into your internal Redfin Agent Dashboard, navigate to the Notable/Reno tab, and enter the active or upcoming listing contract details to request a design quote.
Agent Payout & Commissions: Standard Redfin listing commission splits apply. Because the renovation increases the final open-market sales price, your gross sales volume and total commission dollar amount are effectively maximized.
MLS Listing Window (Days): N/A. Upfront renovation timeline occurs prior to hitting the active market, with the balance floated until final retail closing.
Resale Profit Policy: 100% of the valuation upside and premium market profit generated by the renovations goes directly into your client's pocket at closing.
Back-End Operational Flow: An internal corporate capital allocation program funded directly by Redfin to accelerate listing velocity and maximize listing competitive advantages on the MLS.
